mnemonics: TREES: TRunk Efficiently REaches Everyone's Shelf
Binary Tree: Buds Interconnected Resulting in Elegant Structure
keyPoints: Definition of a Tree: A binary tree is a data structure in which each node has at most two children (i.e., left child and right child).

Properties of Binary Trees: All nodes in a binary tree have a unique key, each node has a maximum of two children, and the left subtree of a node contains only the left children of its ancestors, and the right subtree of a node contains only the right children of its ancestors.
Types of Binary Tree Traversals: Inorder, Preorder, and Postorder Traversals to visit nodes in a tree in a systematic way.
Threaded Binary Trees: A threaded binary tree is a binary tree where each node has a child pointer that points to its leftmost child, making it easier to traverse the tree.

Threaded Binary Tree Traversal: Traversing a threaded binary tree can be done using a stack or recursion, where the stack stores the nodes to be visited and the recursion stores the nodes to be visited and the parent node.
